target.title: Document curated public-corpus skill exceptions
target.body:
## Summary

This PR documents the trust boundary for curated public-corpus skill exceptions without adding an installable skill fixture.

- keeps direct skill additions rejected unless maintainers explicitly approve the curated public-corpus exception
- documents the exception criteria and required evidence in `specs/dev-seeding.md`
- clarifies that public-corpus dummy seeded owners are fixture-local and not publisher provenance
- removes the proposed `coe-root-cause` corpus row/readable fixture/sync test from this PR, so no skill bypasses owner-scoped publishing

## Trust / security impact

Normal community or publisher skills must still use the authenticated owner-scoped publish path. A committed public-corpus skill fixture is only allowed when maintainers explicitly approve the exception and the PR includes readable source, sync proof, validation, and rationale.

This branch no longer lands `coe-root-cause` as public-corpus content. It only records the policy needed for maintainers to evaluate future curated fixture exceptions.

## Verification

Current head: `fcb065928277096bb941e6af5f03f0603a3de35e`

```console
$ bun run validate:public-corpus
$ bun scripts/public-corpus/validate-public-corpus.ts
{
  "ok": true,
  "fixture": "fixtures/public-corpus/corpus.jsonl",
  "rowCount": 1250,
  "skillCount": 1000,
  "pluginCount": 250
}
```

```console
$ bun test scripts/public-corpus/validate.test.ts scripts/public-corpus/seed-public-corpus.test.ts
bun test v1.3.14 (0d9b296a)

scripts/public-corpus/seed-public-corpus.test.ts:
(pass) public corpus seed runner > recognizes retryable Convex write conflicts [0.48ms]
(pass) public corpus seed runner > retries retryable batch conflicts before succeeding [1.39ms]
(pass) public corpus seed runner > does not retry non-conflict failures [0.04ms]

scripts/public-corpus/validate.test.ts:
(pass) public corpus fixture validation > accepts clean skill and plugin rows [0.74ms]
(pass) public corpus fixture validation > rejects owner identity fields and raw Convex ids [0.22ms]
(pass) public corpus fixture validation > rejects duplicate slugs within each artifact kind [0.32ms]
(pass) public corpus fixture validation > rejects empty content and secret-like text [0.05ms]
(pass) public corpus fixture validation > rejects raw local absolute paths but allows redacted path placeholders [0.05ms]
(pass) public corpus fixture validation > parses newline-delimited corpus rows [0.18ms]
(pass) public corpus fixture validation > documents the committed fixture path

 10 pass
 0 fail
 22 expect() calls
Ran 10 tests across 2 files. [95.00ms]
```

```console
$ bun run ci:static
$ bun run check:peers && bun audit --ignore GHSA-rmmr-r34h-pfm5 && bun run format:check && bun run lint && bun run deadcode:ci
$ bun scripts/check-peer-deps.ts

bun audit v1.3.14 (0d9b296a)
No vulnerabilities found
$ oxfmt --check
Checking formatting...
All matched files use the correct format.
Finished in 860ms on 845 files using 10 threads.
$ bun run lint:oxlint
$ oxlint --type-aware --tsconfig ./tsconfig.oxlint.json ./src ./convex ./packages/clawhub/src ./packages/clawhub-mod/src ./packages/schema/src
$ bun run deadcode:knip
$ bun run deadcode:files && bun run deadcode:dependencies && bun run deadcode:exports
$ bunx knip@6.8.0 --config knip.config.ts --production --no-progress --reporter compact --files --no-config-hints
$ bunx knip@6.8.0 --config knip.config.ts --production --no-progress --reporter compact --dependencies --no-config-hints
$ KNIP_INCLUDE_TESTS=1 bunx knip@6.8.0 --config knip.config.ts --no-progress --reporter compact --exports --no-config-hints
```
